title: mentha aquatica var. umbrosa
intro: Mentha aquatica var. umbrosa, also known as Corsican mint, is a variety of the water mint plant, native to Europe and widely cultivated for its medicinal and culinary properties. It is prized for its minty aroma and flavor, which is derived from the essential oils present in its leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory problems due to its expectorant properties. The leaves of Mentha aquatica var. umbrosa can be consumed as a tea, added to salads or desserts, or used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and essential oil extracts to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
